question.text: What power does Section 11C of the SEBI Act confer on SEBI?

answer.text: Power to direct investigation into affairs of intermediaries or persons associated with securities market when transactions are detrimental to investors
Power to cancel registration of intermediaries
Power to levy monetary penalties on listed companies
Power to regulate issue of prospectus and offer documents

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Section 11C, when SEBI has reasonable ground to believe that transactions in securities are being dealt with in a manner detrimental to investors or the securities market, or that any intermediary has violated SEBI Act provisions, SEBI may by written order direct any person to investigate the affairs of such intermediary. **The Trap:** Registration cancellation and penalty levying are under different sections. Prospectus regulation is under Section 11A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Section 11C is the investigation trigger — it authorizes SEBI to order formal investigations when violations are suspected.
